title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss joined the Patriots for a fourth-round pick in 2007, achieving an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in a season.
Wes Welker, acquired from the Dolphins in 2007, became a prolific slot receiver complementing Randy Moss in New England.
Brandin Cooks had a productive 2017 season but was traded after one year; his impact was limited by his short tenure.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora was ejected during the eighth inning of the 6-5 loss against the Reds after arguing a strike call.
Trevor Story disagreed with a check swing strike three call, which was not referred to the first base umpire.
Cora emphasized learning from the incident and focusing on Automatic Ball-Strike (ABS) system strategy for future games.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story criticized umpire C.B. Bucknor for a strike three call in the Red Sox's 6-5 loss.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora was ejected after arguing the call against Story during a crucial at-bat.
Despite Story's early home run, the Red Sox lost in 11 innings and are now 1-1 for the season.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha scored 11 goals in 16 games since the Olympic break after missing the event due to a concussion.
Zacha set a career-high with 26 goals this season, needing four more points to surpass his best single-season points total.
Boston's second line, featuring Zacha, Viktor Arvidsson, and Casey Mittelstadt, has outscored opponents 37-16 in 5-on-5 play.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray lasted four innings in his Red Sox debut against the Cincinnati Reds, allowing four runs on 80 pitches.
Manager Alex Cora said Gray settled in after a rough start and praised the bullpen's performance in the 6-5 loss.
Justin Slaten allowed a walk-off hit in the 11th inning as the Red Sox failed to secure a lead during the game.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 150,000 people attended the third 'No Kings' protest at Boston Common, featuring speeches and performances.
Governor Maura Healey and Senator Elizabeth Warren addressed the rally, criticizing political and military actions.
Organizers set up 'action tables' for attendees to join advocacy groups like ACLU Massachusetts and Indivisible Mass Coalition.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 90-minute, 17-song set at Boston's House of Blues, featuring shock rock theatrics and fake blood.
The band covered Chappell Roan's "Pink Pony Club" and paid tribute to late member Dave Brockie in the encore.
Their set included tracks like "Saddam a Go-Go" and "Metal Metal Land," with on-stage parody of various figures.

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum are among four Celtics listed as questionable for Sunday against the Hornets.
Nikola Vucevic is confirmed out for the Celtics due to a right ring finger fracture.
Jayson Tatum has been struggling with shooting despite recording high minutes and contributing significantly in games.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands gathered on Boston Common for the nationwide 'No Kings' protest, with over 160 rallies across Massachusetts.
Speakers included Gov. Maura Healey and Sen. Elizabeth Warren, with performances by the Dropkick Murphys.
Protesters raised concerns about the country's future, democracy, and advocated against concentrated power and monarchy.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands attended the 'No Kings' protest in Boston Common on March 28, 2026, joining over 160 statewide events.
Governor Maura Healey and Senators Edward Markey and Elizabeth Warren addressed the crowd at the Boston protest.
The protest is part of a nationwide movement highlighting dissatisfaction with current governmental structures.
